Brief Description: Circomscribe is a tool showing what lines of a given Circom file produce what constraints (if any). Essentially, Circomscribe is a modified version of the Circom compiler running in WebAssembly (Wasm) and reporting information in a JSON format. Albeit interesting, Circomscribe is not actually a security analysis tool, but rather a simple code inspector.
CM is similar to CMDCGD and CMS, but with a specific focus on smart contracts
zkwasm-fv focuses on verifying a zkVM
fAmulet addresses finalization problems specific for layer 2 smart contracts (e.g., roll-up)
OWBB is implemented in the CirC ZKP compiler
TSWCTS has no implementation, but concerns a methodology for auditing
Distiller is a verified compiler more focused on construction of correct frontends
AGLST is a verified compiler using the (old) Lean3 proof assistant
Tabby is a verfied compiler (actually, translation-validation based)
SCLL is a foundational result for static-analysis
PinocchioQ is a verified compiler (C to R1CS)
POPBPDB is a foundational result for SMT
MTZK is a compiler tester and does not directly focuses on ZKP circuits or programs
OPBFBD is a foundational result for SMT
